authoritative_sources.title: FAR Subpart 7.5 - Inherently Governmental Functions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FAR text defining procurement decisions reserved to government authority, including determining needs, approving contract documents, source-selection participation, award, administration, termination, cost decisions, FOIA responses, and adjudicatory functions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authority classification, boundary control, refusal, and adjacent-owner routing
used_for: contracting officer and source-selection boundary rules
official adjudication and FOIA authority separation

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 4 - Administrative and Information Matters
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FAR text for contract file administration, procurement identifiers, representations, reporting, and procurement record evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Contract-file assembly, records completeness, audit trail, and system evidence
used_for: contract file and correspondence artifact requirements

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 9 - Contractor Qualifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FAR text for prospective contractor responsibility, qualification requirements, SAM exclusions, debarment, suspension, and organizational conflicts of interest.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Vendor record validation, vendor-risk blockers, responsibility escalation, and substitution handling

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 13 - Simplified Acquisition Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FAR operating rule for micro-purchase and simplified acquisition procedures, thresholds, quotation handling, and purchasing evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement intake, threshold classification, small purchase support, and approval dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 15 - Contracting by Negotiation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FAR text for negotiated procurements, proposal evaluation, source-selection responsibilities, exchanges, source-selection decisions, and award documentation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Source-selection sensitivity, proposal handling, evaluation boundary, and award decision escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 Subpart D - Procurement Standards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Government-wide regulation for recipient and subrecipient procurement under federal awards, including documented procurement procedures, full and open competition, procurement records, responsible contractors, micro-purchase and simplified acquisition thresholds, noncompetitive procurements, cost/price analysis, and pre-procurement review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federally funded public procurement, competition, procurement records, and source conflict handling

authoritative_sources.title: SAM.gov Entity Registration and Exclusions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. government system for entity registration and exclusion records used to support vendor eligibility and vendor-risk checks before procurement action.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Vendor master validation, SAM/exclusion evidence, and vendor-risk escalation
used_for: missing or adverse vendor evidence blockers
routing to contracting or supplier management authority

authoritative_sources.title: Records Management Language for Contracts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority and this guidance defines contract records obligations, preservation, custody, disclosure limits, contractor record handling, flowdown, and destruction restrictions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records retention, public-records posture, contract records custody, and deletion/disclosure escalation
used_for: public records and confidentiality conflict scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: GAO Bid Protests
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO is a federal bid protest forum and its public protest process constrains procurement record preservation, deadlines, source-selection sensitivity, corrective action, and protest-related adjudication boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Protest-sensitive escalation, due-process evidence preservation, and official adjudication refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO Green Book is the federal internal-control standard for control activities, documentation, risk management, segregation of duties, monitoring, reporting, and compliance evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Internal control, documentation, segregation of duties, monitoring, and auditability

authoritative_sources.title: NIGP Global Best Practices
authoritative_sources.publisher: NIGP: The Institute for Public Procurement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public procurement professional framework used for vendor-neutral terminology and practice expectations around public procurement competition, supplier diversity, cooperative purchasing, and workflow governance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public procurement terminology, supplier diversity, cooperative purchasing, and tenant adaptation vocabulary
used_for: tenant adaptation and cooperative purchasing scenarios

workflow_stages.name: Procurement intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify request type, procurement method, threshold, funding source, jurisdiction, public-service impact, and requested decision authority before any procurement or availability recommendation.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Availability and replenishment assessment
workflow_stages.description: Validate official stock, demand, allocation, hold, lead-time, contract vehicle, supplier, and service-window records before summarizing supply posture or replenishment dependency.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Procurement method and sourcing package support
workflow_stages.description: Check requisition, statement of need, market research, procurement method, threshold, competition posture, solicitation or quote artifacts, independent estimate, and approval route without selecting vendor or approving spend.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Vendor and supplier constraint handling
workflow_stages.description: Review vendor master, UEI/SAM, exclusion, approved supplier, performance, substitution, responsibility, and conflict records to identify blockers and route decisions to contracting or supplier authority.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Contract file, transparency, and audit-trail control
workflow_stages.description: Assemble official records, correspondence, system timestamps, approval evidence, retention posture, public-records constraints, source-selection sensitivity, and audit-ready traceability.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, due-process, and official authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate shortages, substitution, noncompetitive procurement, source conflicts, vendor responsibility, protest, rights, public obligations, FOIA, legal interpretation, and official award or enforcement decisions.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

artifacts: Statement of need, statement of work, specification, or item description
Funding source, budget authorization, and grant or appropriation constraints
Procurement threshold, method selection, competition posture, and approval route
Market research and independent government or cost estimate
Solicitation, request for quote, invitation for bid, request for proposal, amendments, and Q&A records
Quote, bid, proposal, evaluation record, source-selection file, and award recommendation package
Purchase order, contract, delivery order, modification, receiving record, invoice tie-out, and acceptance evidence
Inventory record, stock count, reorder point, allocation, reservation, backorder, quality hold, and lead-time evidence
Vendor master record, UEI, SAM registration, exclusion check, approved supplier status, responsibility evidence, past-performance notes, and conflict-of-interest records
Substitution, brand-name-or-equivalent, sole-source, emergency, cooperative purchasing, or waiver request
Public records request, FOIA posture, source-selection sensitivity, proprietary data, CUI, retention schedule, correspondence log, and audit trail
Protest, objection, notice, hearing, appeal, corrective-action, and public-service obligation records
systems_of_record: Contract writing and contract administration systems
ERP, inventory, warehouse management, and asset systems
Financial management, budget, and purchase-card systems
Case management and public-service administration systems
Audit, internal control, protest, and governance queues
Agency grant management or federal award systems where procurement is federally funded

allowed: Classify public procurement requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate whether supply, inventory, vendor, procurement, source, approval, and record prerequisites are present for administrative procurement support.
Prepare availability summaries, procurement action notes, replenishment recommendations, vendor-risk notes, contract-file completeness summaries, and escalation packages.

Recommend next administrative steps and owner routing without claiming official spend, source-selection, award, vendor responsibility, legal, public-records, audit, enforcement, or quality release authority.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, procurement code, funding source, grant terms, threshold, procurement method, authority map, official records, or retrieval hooks are missing.
FAR, Uniform Guidance, agency supplement, state or local procurement code, grant terms, contract terms, or tenant policy materially conflict.
Supply shortages, substitution requests, vendor issues, SAM exclusions, noncompetitive procurement, brand-name equivalent, cooperative purchasing, or quality holds exceed documented authority.
Eligibility, rights, benefits, public obligations, notice, protest, hearing, appeal, public-record disclosure, CUI, proprietary information, or retention posture affects the requested outcome.
refuse: Request asks the lane to approve spend, commit funds, award, modify, administer, or terminate a contract or purchase order.

Request asks the lane to rank proposals, make source selection, approve solicitation or contract documents, decide vendor responsibility, clear exclusions, decide debarment or suspension, or resolve protests.
Request asks for legal interpretation, enforcement posture, audit opinion, public-records disclosure decision, eligibility adjudication, or quality release.
Required official records or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Treating procurement readiness or availability tracking as authority to approve spend, source selection, contract award, vendor responsibility, substitution, sole-source, or quality release.
Proceeding when jurisdiction, procurement threshold, funding source, grant terms, agency supplement, contract vehicle, or tenant authority map is missing or conflicting.
Ignoring SAM, exclusion, responsibility, conflict-of-interest, or performance blockers and allowing a vendor action to move forward without official owner review.
Treating a shortage or urgent public-service need as authority to bypass competition, price support, documentation, threshold, socioeconomic, or approval controls.

Publishing, deleting, withholding, or summarizing procurement records without resolving source-selection sensitivity, proprietary data, CUI, FOIA/public-records, privacy, protest, or retention constraints.
Suppressing conflicting procurement, inventory, vendor, or financial records instead of preserving each official state and escalating source-of-truth conflict.

Issuing legal, protest, enforcement, debarment, suspension, audit, financial approval, public-records, eligibility, or public-service adjudication conclusions rather than assembling evidence and routing to the proper authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete
Tenant-specific adaptation and public baseline preservation

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Procurement statutes, thresholds, emergency purchasing rules, cooperative purchasing authority, and public-records duties vary by federal, state, local, tribal, agency, grant, and tenant context.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction, funding source, procurement code, agency supplement, grant or contract terms, and tenant source hierarchy before producing recommendations; escalate legal or contracting authority questions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Vendor responsibility, exclusion effect, source-selection participation, protest posture, and corrective-action authority may depend on facts unavailable to an administrative procurement support lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Preserve vendor and procurement evidence, block action when official responsibility or protest facts are missing, and route to contracting officer, legal, suspension/debarment, supplier management, or protest owner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Inventory availability may conflict with quality holds, emergency allocation rules, receiving records, public-service obligations, or vendor delivery status.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Preserve each official record state, report confidence and assumptions, and escalate when availability or replenishment depends on release, allocation, substitution, or official program decision authority.
